word: G2384 - Ἰακώβ - Iakṓb - ee-ak-obe' - Also an Israelite:--Jacob

sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ἰακώβ

- Transliteration: Iakob

- Phonetic: ee-ak-obe'

- Definition: Jacob = "heel-catcher or supplanter"

1. was the second son of Isaac

2. the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary

- Origin: of hebrew origin H3290

- TDNT entry: *,344

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of hebrew origin [H3290]; Jacob (that is Ja'akob) the pogenitor of the Israelites; also an Israelite: - Jacob.
